summary: iter31: Full verification of Razorpay payment-failure handling on top of iter30 subscription rebuild. Backend: 8/8 pytest tests PASSED covering (1) subscription.pending webhook → status='pending' + ONE dedup notification (sub_pending:{sid}), (2) subscription.halted → status='halted' + ONE dedup notification (sub_halted:{sid}), (3) payment.failed on one-time order → payment_orders.status='failed' + failure_reason stored + 'Payment failed' notification, (4) payment.failed does NOT notify when order was already paid, (5) admin-only GET /admin/qa/failed-subscriptions returns halted+pending with user enrichment and rejects non-admin with 403, (6) regression subscription.charged still materialises purchase idempotently and does NOT fire failure notification, (7) regression /subscription/init after halted returns a FRESH subscription_id. Frontend user: /app/subscriptions correctly shows red banner + red 'Retry with new mandate' button on halted card, HIDES Cancel button, and clicking Retry opens the SubscriptionCheckoutModal for the same program_id. Frontend admin: /admin/qa/live-check shows the new 'Failed subscriptions' card with count badge '6 halted/pending', per-row program name + status chip + user name/email + subscription_id, refresh button reloads the list. All data-testids are present and correct.
action_items: None required — payment-failure handling is production-ready. RAZORPAY_MOCK_MODE has been restored to false in /app/backend/.env after tests.
critical_code_review_comments: app/services/notify.py: subscription_renewal_pending() and subscription_halted() correctly use dedup_key='sub_pending:{sid}' / 'sub_halted:{sid}'. Both use category='payment' and cta_link='/app/subscriptions' as spec'd. notify() dedup check queries meta.dedup_key with deleted_at=None — correct.

app/routes/payments.py:_handle_subscription_webhook — failure notif branch is gated on event ∈ (pending, halted); mapping in new_status dict correctly translates event → status. Notify call is wrapped in try/except so a notification insert failure will not fail the webhook (best-effort pattern).

app/routes/payments.py: payment.failed branch correctly guards on order_doc.get('status') not in ('paid','failed') — prevents overwriting a successful capture AND prevents double-notify on webhook replay. failure_reason falls back to error_description → error_reason → ''.

app/routes/qa.py: GET /admin/qa/failed-subscriptions properly filters status ∈ {halted, pending}, sorts by updated_at desc, enriches with users(full_name, email, mobile) and drops _id. Admin-only via get_current_admin dependency (401/403 for non-admin).

frontend/src/pages/MySubscriptions.jsx: halted status is NOT in TERMINAL set so halted subs appear in Active list (correct — user still has current-cycle access). Card renders red banner + red Retry button; Cancel button is hidden via `!isTerminal && !isHalted` guard. Retry opens SubscriptionCheckoutModal with the sub's program_id; on success, list is reloaded.
frontend/src/components/SubscriptionCheckoutModal.jsx: pollVerify timeout copy updated to the new friendly message about UPI approval / bank decline / timeout — verified present on line 94.

frontend/src/pages/AdminLiveCheck.jsx: Failed subscriptions card sits between coverage checklist and recent events (visual ordering confirmed via screenshot). Row shows program name, status chip, user name/email, monthly frequency, sub id.
updated_files: /app/backend/.env (RAZORPAY_MOCK_MODE flipped false→true for tests, RESTORED to false)
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_iter31_* subscription programs and TEST_iter31ui_Halted_Program used by the UI test. Halted sub mock_sub_b271a1d7aa8b4efd left in qa-tester's active list for admin card verification. Multiple test_iter31_*@example.com dummy users. Cleanup available via admin danger zone.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Payment-failure handling is fully verified. Notifications use /api/notifications/me (NOT /api/notifications) and records use `user_membership_id` field. Backend .env RAZORPAY_MOCK_MODE=false (restored). To reproduce in mock mode: flip to true + restart backend + run pytest backend/tests/test_iter31_payment_failure.py. Halted subs are kept in the Active section (not Past) because the user retains current-cycle access — this is intentional per spec 3a. The retry flow creates a fresh Razorpay subscription_id (reuse logic skips terminal-status rows including halted).
verified_flows: Backend: subscription.pending webhook (fired twice) → status='pending' + exactly ONE notification with dedup_key=sub_pending:{sid}, title='Renewal payment failed', category='payment', cta_link='/app/subscriptions'

Backend: subscription.halted webhook (fired twice) → status='halted' + exactly ONE notification with dedup_key=sub_halted:{sid}, title='Auto-renewal stopped', category='payment', cta_link='/app/subscriptions'
Backend: payment.failed webhook on unpaid order → payment_orders.status='failed', failure_reason stored, 'Payment failed' notification created
Backend: payment.failed webhook on already-paid order → no new failure notification created (guard works)
Backend: GET /admin/qa/failed-subscriptions with admin token → 200 with items containing user sub-object (full_name, email, mobile); non-admin → 401/403
Backend regression: subscription.charged creates purchase idempotently (2 webhook fires → 1 purchase row); no failure notification fired on success
Backend regression: /payments/subscription/init after halted mandate returns fresh subscription_id, reused=false

Frontend user: qa-tester logs in, /app/subscriptions renders halted card with 'Payment failed' status chip, red banner 'Auto-renewal stopped after multiple failed charges', red 'Retry with new mandate' button; Cancel button absent
Frontend user: clicking Retry opens SubscriptionCheckoutModal with the halted sub's program_id and mock-mode 'Subscribe · ₹199' button

Frontend admin: /admin/qa/live-check shows Failed subscriptions card (data-testid=live-check-failed-subs-card) between coverage checklist and recent events cards. Count badge '6 halted/pending', per-row program name + halted/pending chip + user name/email + monthly frequency + sub id. Refresh button reloads the list
